Therapist: You're thinking that maybe you can apply the same calm approach to riding out your urge to smoke, like you did with the presentation. Can you imagine what would happen if you were able to stay calm and ride out one urge to smoke without giving in?

Patient: ..if I could just do it once, I think it might give me the confidence to try it again. And again. Maybe it would start to feel more normal not to smoke, you know? Like, I don't know, maybe I could start to feel like I can handle the urges, instead of feeling so out of control.

Therapist: Now that you're thinking about the confidence boost that can come from riding out one urge to smoke, how would you like to start practicing this "urge surfing" technique in a low-stakes situation, like when you're not feeling as anxious or stressed?
